headline: MediaTek eyes Google TPU and Musk AI rack deals in system-level pivot - Kuo
summary: "My latest industry checks indicate that MediaTek has upgraded the strategic positioning of its AI business from 'IC / ASIC design' to 'system-level design,' initially targeting the PCBA (L6) for Google's TPU and the L10 rack for Elon Musk-affiliated companies' in-house AI chips," Kuo wrote. The analyst frames the move as a calculated long-term repositioning rather than a near-term earnings catalyst, noting that it carries "immaterial impact on fundamentals over the next two years."

content.title: MediaTek eyes Google TPU and Musk AI rack deals in system-level pivot - Kuo
content.summary: "My latest industry checks indicate that MediaTek has upgraded the strategic positioning of its AI business from 'IC / ASIC design' to 'system-level design,' initially targeting the PCBA (L6) for Google's TPU and the L10 rack for Elon Musk-affiliated companies' in-house AI chips," Kuo wrote. The analyst frames the move as a calculated long-term repositioning rather than a near-term earnings catalyst, noting that it carries "immaterial impact on fundamentals over the next two years."

headline: If You Put $500 a Month into This Tech ETF Since 2016, You Could Retire Tomorrow

headline: What Is The Market Really Expecting From PLTR Stock?
summary: At $127.99, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is being priced to deliver 33.3% revenue growth annually for the next 7 years simply to defend today's 134.3x multiple. That sits below the 67.7% the business is currently growing at, which is the unusual part. The multiple has already priced in a slowdown that has not yet appeared.

content.title: What Is The Market Really Expecting From PLTR Stock?
content.summary: At $127.99, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is being priced to deliver 33.3% revenue growth annually for the next 7 years simply to defend today's 134.3x multiple. That sits below the 67.7% the business is currently growing at, which is the unusual part. The multiple has already priced in a slowdown that has not yet appeared.

headline: Musk’s SpaceX $1 Trillion Revenue Goal
summary: Elon Musk thinks SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) will have $1 trillion in revenue in 2031. Its revenue last year was just shy of $19 billion. According to CNBC, Goldman Sachs forecasts $470 in 2030.
